KNES - Kinesiology

KNES400 The Foundations of Public Health in Kinesiology (3 Credits) An investigation of the role of physical activity and inactivity in relation to health and well-being through a public health perspective. Past and current perspectives on health promotion, health education, and social policies and approaches will be examined for various populations. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in KNES287; and minimum grade of C- in SPHL100.

Restriction: Must be in a major within the SPHL-Kinesiology department; and must have earned a minimum of 75 credits.
